name: Hall v. Hall
question: Should a Supreme Court decision regarding jurisdiction in multidistrict cases be extended to single district consolidated cases, such that the entry of a final judgment in only one case makes that case immediately appealable despite the existence of remaining pending claims in another related case?
description: A case in which the Court decided that when one of multiple cases consolidated under FRCP 42(a) is finally decided, that ruling confers upon the losing party the right to immediate appeal regardless of whether any of the other consolidated cases are still pending.
